summary:
The primary concern is minimizing delay for RBC delivery post-blood bank receipt; thus, using an appropriate gauge like 20-22G ensures efficient transfusions within recommended timeframes while reducing risks associated with slower flowrates via finer-gauge alternatives. Key literature supports this approach over others mentioned. Sentence count: 7
The summary has been condensed into two critical sentences focusing only on essential aspects related directly back to whether RBCs need immediate transfer based upon optimal timing considerations highlighted across multiple reliable medical resources referenced here today.
